I agree with the previous conclusion. In women on anticonvulsants, especially those with higher teratogenic risks like valproate or phenytoin, preconceptional folic acid supplementation—starting three months before pregnancy—is crucial because it ensures sufficient folate levels during the first few weeks of gestation when neural tube closure occurs, which may occur before a woman knows she’s pregnant.
